question: How many patients are in the database?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_patients FROM patients;

question: Which doctors work in Cardiology?
expected_sql: SELECT d.first_name, d.last_name FROM doctors d JOIN departments dep ON d.department_id = dep.department_id WHERE dep.department_name = 'Cardiology' ORDER BY d.last_name, d.first_name;

question: How many appointments are in the database?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_appointments FROM appointments;

question: Which appointments were completed?
expected_sql: SELECT appointment_id, appointment_date, reason, diagnosis FROM appointments WHERE status = 'completed' ORDER BY appointment_date DESC, appointment_id DESC;

question: Which medications were prescribed?
expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT medication_name FROM prescriptions ORDER BY medication_name;

question: Which patients received Paracetamol?
expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.first_name, p.last_name FROM patients p JOIN prescriptions pr ON p.patient_id = pr.patient_id WHERE pr.medication_name = 'Paracetamol' ORDER BY p.last_name, p.first_name;

question: How many appointments did each doctor have?
expected_sql: SELECT d.first_name, d.last_name, COUNT(a.appointment_id) AS total_appointments FROM doctors d LEFT JOIN appointments a ON d.doctor_id = a.doctor_id GROUP BY d.doctor_id, d.first_name, d.last_name ORDER BY total_appointments DESC, d.last_name, d.first_name;

question: How many appointments took place in each department?
expected_sql: SELECT dep.department_name, COUNT(a.appointment_id) AS total_appointments FROM departments dep LEFT JOIN appointments a ON dep.department_id = a.department_id GROUP BY dep.department_id, dep.department_name ORDER BY total_appointments DESC, dep.department_name;

question: Which doctor had the most appointments?
expected_sql: SELECT d.first_name, d.last_name, COUNT(a.appointment_id) AS total_appointments FROM doctors d JOIN appointments a ON d.doctor_id = a.doctor_id GROUP BY d.doctor_id, d.first_name, d.last_name ORDER BY total_appointments DESC LIMIT 1;

question: Which patients have more than one prescription?
expected_sql: SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, COUNT(pr.prescription_id) AS total_prescriptions FROM patients p JOIN prescriptions pr ON p.patient_id = pr.patient_id GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.first_name, p.last_name HAVING COUNT(pr.prescription_id) > 1 ORDER BY total_prescriptions DESC, p.last_name, p.first_name;

question: Show appointments in Cardiology.
expected_sql: SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, a.appointment_date, a.reason FROM appointments a JOIN patients p ON a.patient_id = p.patient_id JOIN departments dep ON a.department_id = dep.department_id WHERE dep.department_name = 'Cardiology' ORDER BY a.appointment_date DESC, p.last_name, p.first_name;

question: Count prescriptions by medication.
expected_sql: SELECT medication_name, COUNT(*) AS total_prescriptions FROM prescriptions GROUP BY medication_name ORDER BY total_prescriptions DESC, medication_name;

question: What are the 5 most recent appointments with patient names?
expected_sql: SELECT a.appointment_date, p.first_name, p.last_name, a.reason FROM appointments a JOIN patients p ON a.patient_id = p.patient_id ORDER BY a.appointment_date DESC, a.appointment_id DESC LIMIT 5;

question: Show all departments ranked by number of completed appointments.
expected_sql: SELECT dep.department_name, COUNT(a.appointment_id) AS total_completed FROM departments dep JOIN appointments a ON dep.department_id = a.department_id WHERE a.status = 'completed' GROUP BY dep.department_id, dep.department_name ORDER BY total_completed DESC, dep.department_name;

question: Which diagnosis appears most frequently in Emergency?
expected_sql: SELECT a.diagnosis, COUNT(*) AS total_cases FROM appointments a JOIN departments dep ON a.department_id = dep.department_id WHERE dep.department_name = 'Emergency' GROUP BY a.diagnosis ORDER BY total_cases DESC, a.diagnosis LIMIT 1;

question: Show patients who visited more than one department and how many departments they visited.
expected_sql: SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT a.department_id) AS total_departments FROM patients p JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.first_name, p.last_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.department_id) > 1 ORDER BY total_departments DESC, p.last_name, p.first_name;

question: Which city has the highest number of patients with at least one appointment?
expected_sql: SELECT p.city, COUNT(DISTINCT p.patient_id) AS total_patients FROM patients p JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id GROUP BY p.city ORDER BY total_patients DESC, p.city LIMIT 1;

question: Which medications are most prescribed for Neurology appointments?
expected_sql: SELECT pr.medication_name, COUNT(*) AS total_prescriptions FROM prescriptions pr JOIN appointments a ON pr.appointment_id = a.appointment_id JOIN departments dep ON a.department_id = dep.department_id WHERE dep.department_name = 'Neurology' GROUP BY pr.medication_name ORDER BY total_prescriptions DESC, pr.medication_name;

question: List the top 5 doctors by number of appointments.
expected_sql: SELECT d.first_name, d.last_name, COUNT(a.appointment_id) AS total_appointments FROM doctors d JOIN appointments a ON d.doctor_id = a.doctor_id GROUP BY d.doctor_id, d.first_name, d.last_name ORDER BY total_appointments DESC, d.last_name, d.first_name LIMIT 5;

question: For each department, show total appointments and distinct patients.
expected_sql: SELECT dep.department_name, COUNT(a.appointment_id) AS total_appointments, COUNT(DISTINCT a.patient_id) AS total_patients FROM departments dep LEFT JOIN appointments a ON dep.department_id = a.department_id GROUP BY dep.department_id, dep.department_name ORDER BY total_appointments DESC, dep.department_name;
